programmes.show_description: Rebecca's tired of guys with bad boy images breaking her heart. Dan thinks the only thing missing in his life's the love of a good man. Can they find love the Naked Attraction way?
Brand new series: Revelations Week escalates with a confrontational task that prompts shocking confessions, brutal opinions and a blazing argument.
Britain's sharpest armchair critics share their insightful and passionate takes on the best TV shows from series 21.
Not in My House: When Claire finds an X-rated picture on the computer and assumes that it's Luke's doing, a guilty Phil doesn't necessarily correct her.
Fifteen Percent: Mitchell seeks revenge when Jay introduces Cameron as just his 'friend'. Gloria gives Manny's date a makeover.
Mom Trumps Willow: Adam decides to see the movie Willow instead of spending Mother's Day with Beverly. Barry is tired of being the middle child.
Craig's grand plan for his wedding to Sofia was to combine his two great loves: her and beer. He threw an Oktoberfest-themed do. What did she really think?
Commencement: Cam finally gets a chance to shine when he's asked to preside over the high school graduation ceremony at the last minute, while Jay gives the commencement address.
A Year of Birthdays: As the birth of Haley and Dylan's twins approaches, the family look back at how each of them celebrated their birthdays this past year.
A short, cheeky clip featuring animals and food, from the always hilarious Tube that is Rude.
The Burger Kitchen (Part 1/2): In the first half of a two-part Nightmare, Gordon tries to save a burger joint in LA, run by a dysfunctional family in a state of constant conflict.
Vacation: Beverly marshals everyone for one last family vacation to Disneyland. Erica worries that Geoff wants to break up. Guest starring Christie Brinkley.
Dana's Back: Adam gets a blast from the past in the shape of Dana - his first love. Meanwhile, Erica and Barry are heading to college with nowhere to live.
Come Fly with Me: Jay reluctantly takes Phil out to fly his new model plane, but a manoeuvre goes awry...
The Incident: Claire and Mitchell's mother, Dede, drops in for a surprise visit and is determined to make amends with everyone who she offended during 'the incident'.
Heartbreak Hotel: Homer and Marge go on a big reality TV competition but are eliminated immediately. And the blame game starts soon after...
My Way or the Highway to Heaven: The Simpsons explore three ways to enter heaven, including Ned's conversion as a trampoline salesman.
Baby You Can't Drive My Car: Marge and Homer work together at a cool tech company that makes self-driving cars. But the cars have a sinister secret app...
Kim Kardashian lookalike Becky seeks a 'manly man' who'll love both her and her snake. Is leather-clad Lee the one? And is Sean the settling-down kind of toy boy that Emma wants?
The Burger Kitchen (Part 2/2): In the second half of a two-part Nightmare in LA, can Gordon Ramsay bring together the fragmented Saffron family and save their failing restaurant?
Kill Gil, Volumes I & II: The Simpsons are stuck with an unwanted house guest, Gil, at Christmas. And he stays for a whole year... until Marge throws him out on Christmas Eve.
The Wife Aquatic: The family visit Barnacle Bay, where Marge grew up, but discover that it's become run-down. Can Homer help to clean up the town?
Moon Landing: Claire feels slightly inadequate when her old friend, Valerie (guest star Minnie Driver), visits and is nothing like Claire remembered.
My Funky Valentine: Phil and Claire break from their traditional Valentine's Day dinner date and plan a little role play excursion. And Jay and Gloria's ideas of romance clash.
Heartbreak Hotel: Homer and Marge go on a big reality TV competition but are eliminated immediately. And the blame game starts soon after...
My Way or the Highway to Heaven: The Simpsons explore three ways to enter heaven, including Ned's conversion as a trampoline salesman.
Baby You Can't Drive My Car: Marge and Homer work together at a cool tech company that makes self-driving cars. But the cars have a sinister secret app...
As the coach winds its way to Grândola in Portugal, Brendan is on the warpath and two newbies hop aboard. And things get spicy when the celebs swap partners!
Crook and Ladder: When members of the Springfield fire department are injured, Homer, Moe, Apu and Skinner volunteer to be temporary firefighters.
Stop or My Dog Will Shoot: After rescuing Homer from a corn maze, Santa's Little Helper is recruited as a police dog and is teamed up with grizzled veteran cop Lou.
Portrait of a Lackey on Fire: Smithers tires of Mr Burns' indifference and finds solace in the arms of affectionate fashion designer Michael de Graaf.
Special Edna (a.k.a. Love and Marking): When Skinner stands up Mrs Krabappel to attend to his mother's whims, Bart nominates her for Teacher of the Year.
After a life-threatening illness, Georgina's back on the dating scene and looking for a rugged man. Kate seeks a Viking while Ashley wants a man who can make a deeper connection.
(2005) Violent action-thriller starring Jason Statham as the tough courier Frank Martin, who's hired to guard a government drugs czar's son. But his mission soon lurches towards disaster.
A Serious Flanders (Part 1/2): In a Fargo-style two-parter, Flanders donates a big bag of money he's found to an orphanage. But some very nefarious characters want it back...
A Serious Flanders (Part 2/2): Flanders is pursued by ruthless gangsters who want their money back. And Homer manages to get himself caught in the crossfire.
TV's most daring dating show. Pansexual Izzy is open to all genders. Dom's looking for a woman who isn't put off by his posh boy charm.
The Goggleboxers watch Welcome to Wrexham, Love & Death, Animals Up Close with Bertie Gregory, Puzzling with Lucy Worsley, BBC News (escaped prisoner), and My Mum, Your Dad.
On the 'Box this time: Strictly Come Dancing, Sex Education, Blankety Blank, Bake Off, Celebrity Race Across the World, Love & Death, Celebrity SAS: Who Dares Wins, and Ambulance.
Can two singletons find love based on naked attraction alone? Student Marlie needs to stay away from bad boys, while Adam from Bradford craves a woman with no secrets.
Breakin': Adam reveals that Barry never completed his community service requirement. Erica plans for her and Geoff to follow The Grateful Dead all summer.
Vacation: Beverly marshals everyone for one last family vacation to Disneyland. Erica worries that Geoff wants to break up. Guest starring Christie Brinkley.
Dana's Back: Adam gets a blast from the past in the shape of Dana - his first love. Meanwhile, Erica and Barry are heading to college with nowhere to live.
Nathan was worried his Mexican Day of the Dead-themed wedding would scare off bride Jenna. Did his do leave her wedding-day dreams dead at the altar?
Roll up, roll up, see physics defied. Wee plastic bricks, build homes in the sky. Can they 'Lego' of their ego so they can compete? Or tumble down with blocks at their feet? #WorldPoetryDay
Brand new series: Aussie celebs and their mates, embark on a big globe-trotting race. Dashing across nations; trials, tribulations. There's really no time to waste.  #WorldPoetryDay
Brand new series: The Chelsea lot head north of the border, where Sam sets his sights on Zeyno. But when a surprise arrival causes disorder, will it push her to say no? #WorldPoetryDay
Brand new series: Whispers turn into a roar. Could Jazz and Inga be something more? A bombshell at dinner derails all the fun. Is a romance over before it's begun?  #WorldPoetryDay
Manager Mike's marketing is out of control, at bijou New York diner The Mixing Bowl. The restaurant's haemorrhaging cash, on its last gasp. Can Gordon Ramsay save its soul? #WorldPoetryDay
Seven years and a million in debt. But you haven't heard the worst of it yet. Who knows what horrors Gordon will discover? Can Peter's Seascape restaurant ever recover? #WorldPoetryDay
The food of love. Isn't that what they say? French cuisine is the romantic way. But The Secret Garden is far from blooming. Business is wilting. And bankruptcy's looming... #WorldPoetryDay
Brand new series: The final couples say I do; will a groom's intentions ring so true? A bride on the aisle is none the wiser; will a man or a woman be there to surprise her? #WorldPoetryDay
Brand new series: Honeymoons over; couples now mingle. At the dinner party; Mel still feels single. Marriages questioned. Opinions collide. Does Danny have eyes for another groom's bride?
(2020) Dave Bautista's a spy who has a wee whine, when he must keep an eye on a fam whose girl of nine is just fine with making him look asinine. Is that a crime? #WorldPoetryDay
(2017) Teenagers in detention, beam into a game dimension. Spencer gets a pleasant shock, when he turns into The Rock. Bethany's taken aback: she has to become Jack Black. #WorldPoetryDay
Mel B, Rylan, Johnny Vaughan. Happy Mondays Bez and Shaun. On the celebs' TV sets: Quiet Place and Nevermets. Chasers and The UnXplained. Danny Dyer's entertained.  #WorldPoetryDay
Teenagers' Come Dine with Me. Scandal at the BBC. All's Fair, Police Interceptors, Elon Musk, The 1%-ers. Race Across the World, Squid Game: never two shows quite the same. #WorldPoetryDay
Chefs pursue a Michelin star. Picture Slam with Alan Carr. Celebs in the jungle trying to be brave. Australian dogs that misbehave. #WorldPoetryDay
